Okay, so I need to predict the outcome of the Norway vs England quarter-final match at the FIFA World Cup 2026. The venue is Miami Stadium in Miami Gardens, and the kickoff time is July 11, 2026, at 9 PM UTC.

First, let me think about both teams' strengths and weaknesses. Norway has been performing well recently, with a strong squad that includes players like Erling Haaland and Martin Odegaard. They have a good balance between youth and experience, which can be an advantage in high-pressure situations. Their playing style is quite attacking, so they might look to control the game from the start.

On the other hand, England has always been one of the top teams in international football. They have a lot of talent across all positions, especially with players like Harry Kane leading the attack and a solid defense with names like John Stones and Kyle Walker. Their midfield is also strong, which can help them control the tempo of the game.

Looking at their historical head-to-head records, England has generally performed better against Norway. However, Norway's recent form might give them an edge in this match. The venue could be a factor too; Miami Stadium is known for its atmosphere, and both teams will need to adapt to playing in front of a potentially partisan crowd.

In terms of tactics, I expect England to focus on their strengths—strong defense and quick counter-attacks. They might try to contain Norway's attacking plays and exploit any gaps left by Norway when they push forward. Norway, being the underdogs, might adopt an aggressive approach, trying to score early to unsettle England.

Considering all these factors, I think the match could be closely contested. Both teams have strong points, but England's experience in major tournaments might give them a slight edge. They might manage the game better and capitalize on any mistakes Norway makes under pressure.

So, predicting the scoreline, it's possible that England edges out Norway with a narrow 2-1 victory. This would mean England advances to the next round.

parsed.one_line_reasoning: England's experience and tactical nous give them a slight edge over an attacking Norway.
